questions.question: What is the default value of `grid-auto-flow`?
questions.explanation: The default value is `row`, which places items left-to-right, then top-to-bottom.

questions.question: What does `grid-auto-flow: column` do?
options: Places items top-to-bottom first, then left-to-right
Places items left-to-right first, then top-to-bottom
questions.explanation: `grid-auto-flow: column` fills the grid vertically first (column-major order), then moves to the next column.

questions.question: What is the effect of `grid-auto-flow: dense`?
options: It fills gaps in the grid by reordering items visually
It makes items render at higher resolution
It doubles the number of items
questions.explanation: `dense` makes the auto-placement algorithm backfill gaps left by earlier items that span multiple tracks. It may reorder items visually.

questions.question: What is an important accessibility concern with `grid-auto-flow: dense`?
options: Dense packing changes visual order but not DOM order, confusing screen reader users
Dense packing breaks keyboard navigation entirely
Screen readers cannot read dense grids
Dense grids are not accessible at all
questions.explanation: `dense` can place later DOM items before earlier ones visually, but the DOM (and thus screen reader) order remains unchanged, which can confuse users.

questions.question: What does `grid-auto-flow: row dense` do?
options: Row-major placement with dense packing (gap filling)
questions.explanation: `row dense` combines row-major placement (default) with dense packing. Items fill left-to-right and gaps are backfilled.

questions.question: Which `grid-auto-flow` value is best for an image gallery where space efficiency is most important?
questions.explanation: `dense` (or `row dense`) is ideal for image galleries because it fills all gaps, maximizing space usage. The visual reordering is acceptable for decorative content.

questions.question: What happens when `grid-auto-flow` is `column` with `repeat(3, 1fr)` and 9 items?
options: Items fill column 1 (top to bottom), then column 2, then column 3
Items fill row 1 (left to right), then row 2, then row 3
Items are placed in reverse order
questions.explanation: With `column` flow, items 1-3 fill the first column, items 4-6 the second, and items 7-9 the third.

questions.question: True or False: `grid-auto-flow: dense` will always place items in DOM order.
options: False — dense may reorder items visually to fill gaps
True — items always follow DOM order
questions.explanation: Dense packing may place later DOM items before earlier ones to fill gaps. Visual order ≠ DOM order.

questions.question: What property controls the size of implicitly created rows in an auto-flow grid?
questions.explanation: `grid-auto-rows` sets the height of rows that are implicitly created by the auto-placement algorithm.

questions.question: In a grid with `grid-auto-flow: column`, what happens when items exceed the container height?
options: Implicit columns are created to the right
questions.explanation: When items exceed the container height with `column` flow, new columns are implicitly created to the right.

questions.question: What is the difference between `dense` and `row dense`?
options: dense uses whichever direction the grid fills; row dense explicitly uses row-major
questions.explanation: `dense` alone inherits the direction from the grid's default flow (row). `row dense` explicitly specifies row-major direction with dense packing.

questions.question: If item 1 spans 2 columns, item 2 is regular, and we use default flow, where does item 2 go?
options: It goes to the next cell after item 1, which is the start of the next row
It goes to column 3 of row 1
It goes into the gap next to item 1
questions.explanation: Without dense, the algorithm places items sequentially in DOM order. Since item 1 occupies 2 columns and row 1 has 3 columns, item 2 starts at the beginning of row 2.

questions.question: Which `grid-auto-flow` mode creates a visual layout most similar to a masonry grid?
options: row dense but with explicit heights
questions.explanation: `dense` packing creates a compact, gap-free layout similar to masonry, especially when combined with varied item sizes.

questions.question: Can you combine `grid-auto-flow` with `grid-auto-columns`?
options: Yes — grid-auto-flow: column plus grid-auto-columns controls implicit column widths
No — grid-auto-flow only works with rows
questions.explanation: `grid-auto-columns` controls the width of implicit columns, which is relevant for `grid-auto-flow: column` where new columns are created automatically.

questions.question: What happens if `grid-auto-flow: row` is set on a grid with no defined columns?
options: Items stack in a single column, creating new rows
questions.explanation: Without defined columns, the grid creates a single-column layout. Items just stack vertically, creating new rows.

questions.question: For a horizontal scrolling timeline component, which grid-auto-flow value is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: A horizontal timeline fills top-to-bottom first, then creates new columns to the right. `grid-auto-flow: column` creates this behavior naturally.

questions.question: What is the main reason NOT to use `dense` on a form or article layout?
options: It reorders fields/articles visually, breaking the expected reading order
Dense mode is not supported in forms
questions.explanation: For content with a logical reading or interaction order (forms, articles), dense packing would visually reorder elements and confuse users.

questions.question: How does `grid-auto-flow` interact with `order` property on items?
options: The order property is applied before auto-placement — items with lower order are placed first
questions.explanation: The `order` property changes the DOM order for auto-placement purposes. Items with lower `order` values are placed first.

questions.question: When using `grid-auto-flow: column dense`, what determines the number of columns?
options: grid-template-columns defines explicit columns; new columns are created implicitly
The number of items determines columns automatically
questions.explanation: `grid-template-columns` defines the explicit column structure. When items exceed these columns with `column dense`, implicit columns are added.

questions.question: What is the most common real-world use case for `grid-auto-flow: dense`?
options: Image galleries and portfolio grids where space efficiency is paramount
questions.explanation: Image galleries and portfolio grids are the most common use case for dense packing, where the visual reordering is acceptable and space efficiency is important.
